frozen_components: symbolic verification engine (first-order logic, Prolog-style backward chaining)
architecture.summary: The system pairs natural language chain-of-thought with symbolic reasoning over knowledge bases. A language model learns from demonstrations containing first-order logic rules and examples, then iteratively reasons over a nonparametric KB using backward chaining (recovered from Prolog-style execution). Symbolic verification gates intermediate steps: the model generates natural-language reasoning, which is checked against KB queries before proceeding. Text (CoT) and symbolic execution (KB queries, verification results) cross the model boundary.
trained_components: language model policy for step-by-step reasoning

biblio.title: Evaluating Step-by-Step Reasoning through Symbolic Verification

claims.statement: LMLP enjoys more than 25% higher accuracy than CoT on length generalization benchmarks even with smaller model sizes
The system learns from demonstrations containing logic rules and corresponding examples to iteratively reason over KBs, recovering Prolog's backward chaining algorithm
Symbolic verification supports automated verification of LMs' outputs
Synthetic datasets were curated containing equivalent (natural, symbolic) data pairs, where symbolic examples contain first-order logic rules and predicates from nonparametric knowledge bases
